applied_optimizations.name: Environment variables for AMD GPU
applied_optimizations.description: Set GPU_MAX_HW_QUEUES=2, HIP_FORCE_DEV_KERNARG=1, HSA_NO_SCRATCH_RECLAIM=1, PYTORCH_TUNABLEOP_ENABLED=1, TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_HIPBLASLT=1

applied_optimizations.name: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high')
applied_optimizations.description: Enable TF32 precision for faster matrix multiplications on AMD MI355X

applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Apply torch.compile with mode='default' to enable inductor optimizations and kernel fusion

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton matmul kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Manual Triton kernels were 4-6x slower than native rocBLAS/hipBLASLt
attempted_but_failed.error: Initial Triton kernel: 5.36ms vs reference 1.19ms (0.22x speedup)

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile mode=max-autotune
attempted_but_failed.reason: High overhead from extensive autotuning during benchmark, 4.49ms with high variance
attempted_but_failed.error: mode=max-autotune resulted in 4.49ms (std 1.12) vs 0.642ms with mode=default

attempted_but_failed.name: Larger block sizes (128x128)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Larger blocks increased latency from 5.36ms to 7.7ms

top_bottlenecks: GEMM operations dominate (3 layers of Linear)

notes: Native rocBLAS/hipBLASLt kernels are extremely well optimized for MI355X
Manual Triton kernels cannot compete with vendor BLAS for large GEMMs
torch.compile with mode='default' automatically selects best backend (native BLAS for GEMM)
Current implementation matches reference performance (0.994x speedup)
torch.compile internally evaluates Triton kernels (shown in autotune logs) but selects optimal backend
Per amd-kernel-optimization skill: 'torch.compile FIRST' is Rule #1 - manual Triton kernels should only be used after compile is working
For this MLP problem, native BLAS is optimal - further gains would require algorithmic changes or fusion across layers
